project.name: Prototype Backup Dive Computer
project.description: Hardware definition for the real prototype backup dive computer using ESP32-S3, ST7567A SPI LCD, MS5837-30BA pressure sensor, UART GPS, piezo disc buzzer, protected 16340 battery, and wireless charging.

warnings: This hardware definition is for prototype development only.
The device is not certified as a life-support or primary dive computer.
Waterproofing, pressure testing, battery safety, sensor calibration, and firmware validation are required before any real dive usage.

mcu.name: ESP32-S3 N16R8 Dev Board
notes: Avoid strapping pins and USB pins for general peripherals.
GPIO numbers are ESP32 GPIO numbers, not physical header pin numbers.

powerRails.name: 3V3
powerRails.description: 3.3V rail for ESP32 logic, MS5837 pressure sensor, and LCD logic.

powerRails.name: 5V
powerRails.description: 5V rail for GPS module and possibly ESP32 dev board 5V input.

notes: Battery voltage must be divided before entering ESP32 ADC.
Do not connect Li-ion battery directly to GPIO.
Qi 5V output must be divided to 3.3V logic level.
Do not connect 5V directly to GPIO2.
Use 100 to 330 ohm series resistor if possible.
Piezo disc will be mounted to action camera housing wall.
I2C bus SDA line.
Can be shared by multiple I2C devices if addresses are unique.
In the real prototype, the ST7567A LCD is SPI, so GPIO5 is mainly used by MS5837.
I2C bus SCL line.
Can be shared by multiple I2C devices if addresses are unique.
In the real prototype, the ST7567A LCD is SPI, so GPIO6 is mainly used by MS5837.
Controls LCD backlight.
PWM dimming may be supported.
If the LCD module already has a backlight resistor, direct GPIO drive may be possible; otherwise add resistor/transistor driver.
LCD Data/Command select pin.
LCD reset pin.
LCD chip select pin.
LCD SPI data input.
LCD module may label this pin as SDA, DIN, or MOSI.
LCD SPI clock.
LCD module may label this pin as SCL, CLK, or SCK.
Controls GPS power through load switch or MOSFET.
Recommended to reduce standby current.
HIGH means GPS power on if using active-high load switch.
ESP32 TX connects to GPS RX.
UART TX/RX must be cross-connected.
ESP32 RX connects to GPS TX.
UART TX/RX must be cross-connected.

components.name: ESP32-S3 N16R8 Dev Board
notes: Prototype board for firmware and hardware validation.
Final PCB may use a more power-optimized ESP32-S3 module design.

purchaseReference.description: 12864 SPI LCD Module 128x64 ST7567A COG Graphic Display

notes: Exact U8g2 constructor must be verified after receiving the LCD module.
Display orientation, contrast, and inversion may need adjustment.

components.name: MS5837-30BA
notes: Used for depth and water temperature measurement.
Breakout board itself must not be directly exposed to water.
Sensor pressure port and sealing structure must be validated.

connections.description: Compass I2C SCL, not connected in initial prototype
Compass I2C SDA, not connected in initial prototype

notes: Actual default baud rate must be verified.
Many GPS modules default to 9600 baud.

backupBatteryPolicy.policy: Allow GPS cold start. Do not feed GPS VBAT from always-on 3.3V because of standby current concerns.

powerControl.policy: Turn GPS on only when surface position fix is needed, then turn it off.

components.name: 12mm Piezo Disc

mechanicalMounting.purpose: Use housing wall as acoustic resonator

notes: Underwater and sealed housing acoustic performance must be tested.
If sound is too weak, consider transistor driver or dedicated buzzer driver.

components.name: Protected 16340 Li-ion
notes: Use protected 16340 cell with over-charge, over-discharge, over-current, and short-circuit protection.
Protection PCB is not a charger.
A proper CC/CV 4.2V Li-ion charger is still required.
Do not solder directly to the cell.
Protected 16340 cells may be longer than unprotected cells; verify holder fit.

components.name: Wireless Charging Receiver Module
notes: Some 4-terminal boards may include battery charging function.
Verify whether the module is only a 5V receiver or includes Li-ion CC/CV charging.
Verify charge termination voltage, system output behavior, and compatibility with protected 16340 cell.

components.name: Li-ion Charger / Power Module
notes: If wireless charging board already includes proper Li-ion charging, a separate charger may not be required.
If not, add a proper Li-ion CC/CV charger module.
Power-path or load-sharing support is preferred to prevent device reset during charging or battery protection events.

components.name: Action Camera Diving Housing
notes: Verify internal fit.
Verify wireless charging through housing wall.
Verify piezo acoustic output through housing wall.
Verify pressure sensor port and sealing.
Perform pressure and waterproof testing.

nets.name: I2C_SDA
notes: Can be shared with other I2C devices if needed.

nets.name: I2C_SCL
notes: Can be shared with other I2C devices if needed.

nets.name: LCD_SPI
notes: LCD is SPI, not I2C.

nets.name: GPS_UART
notes: GPS TX connects to ESP32 RX GPIO16.
GPS RX connects to ESP32 TX GPIO15.
Use divider because ESP32 GPIO is not 5V tolerant.
Divider ratio is approximately 1/3.
Consider switching the divider off during sleep to reduce standby current.

pinsToAvoid.reason: UART0 TX / logging or programming conflict
UART0 RX / logging or programming conflict
Board-dependent RGB LED or special function
Board-dependent RGB LED or special function

notes: Wokwi simulation may not exactly match the real prototype display.
Wokwi currently uses I2C OLED, while real prototype uses SPI LCD.
Pin map difference must be documented to avoid confusion.

items: Does the ST7567A module work reliably at 3.3V?
Does the backlight pin include a current-limiting resistor?
Which U8g2 constructor matches this module?
Is display inversion or rotation required?
What is the actual I2C address of the MS5837 module?
Are the I2C pull-ups connected to 3.3V?
How will the pressure port be sealed?
Is GPS UART logic level 3.3V?
What is the default baud rate?
How long does cold start take without coin-cell backup?
Does power cycling through load switch work reliably?
Is the wireless charging board a 5V receiver only or does it include Li-ion charging?
What is the charge termination voltage?
Does system output remain stable during charging?
Does it support power-path or load sharing?
Does the protected 16340 physically fit the holder and housing?
What is the real capacity?
What is the actual sleep current?
Can all parts fit inside the action camera housing?
Can wireless charging work through the housing?
Is the piezo sound loud enough through the housing?
Can the pressure sensor be exposed to pressure while electronics remain sealed?

nextSteps: After parts arrive, verify each open question and update this JSON.
Consider generating include/hardware_pins.h from firmwareDefines in the future.
